text:
Once upon a time, in a quirky little town called Oddsville, there lived a wise old owl named Harold. He didn't do much besides sleep and watch the clock tick. But one peculiar morning, he felt like chatting.

Harold decided to visit Mrs. Tweedy's place. She was known for her feathered dolls. There she was, seating her collection on her window sill. Harold had never seen anything like them.

"You're something special," he muttered.

Mrs. Tweedy smiled warmly.

"Why, thank you! They're favorites."

"What bring you that stuff?"

"Well, dear owl."

Harold perched. "Life."

She laughed. "Just live."

He blinked. "That's hard."

"Oh, yes." She picked one up. "It's delicate."

"How'd you feel?"

"Sober."

"I do."

Harold thought. Then he had an idea. "Anything."

"A bird feeder?"

"That'd do."
